docs.title: Before you begin
docs.text:
• Written consent must be obtained and documented
  • Baseline vital signs must be recorded
  • Adrenaline 0.5 mg/mL (1:1000) must be drawn and labelled before commencing any IDT or challenge
  • Positive control (histamine 10 mg/mL) and negative control (normal saline) must be included for all SPT
  • IDT volume: 0.05 mL unless otherwise specified

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Field Detail Drug class Synthetic opioid analgesic Stock vial 1 mg/2 mL (0.5 mg/mL) Reconstituted conc. N/A (Ready to use) Storage Room temp; Protect from light

docs.title: Preparation
docs.text:
Before you start

Ensure the vial contains 1 mg/2 mL (0.5 mg/mL). Check expiry and integrity. Note that Alfentanil is a Schedule 8 (S8) medication and requires appropriate handling and witnessing.

docs.title: Equipment needed
docs.text:
• 1 × Alfentanil 1 mg/2 mL vial
  • Normal saline (NS) — for IDT dilutions
  • 1 mL syringes × 3 (for test doses)
  • 25 G needles

docs.title: Step 1 — Prepare the SPT solution (0.5 mg/mL — neat)
docs.text:
1. Draw up 0.5–1 mL of the stock (0.5 mg/mL) into a 1 mL syringe.
  2. Label: "Alfentanil SPT 0.5 mg/mL".

docs.title: Step 2 — Prepare IDT dilutions
docs.text:
Prepare in this order, using a fresh syringe for each step.

  1. IDT 0.05 mg/mL (1:10):

    • Draw 0.1 mL of stock (0.5 mg/mL) into a 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.05 mg/mL — 1:10).
    • Label: "Alfentanil IDT 0.05 mg/mL".

  2. IDT 0.005 mg/mL (1:100):

    • Draw 0.1 mL of the 0.05 mg/mL syringe into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.005 mg/mL — 1:100).
    • Label: "Alfentanil IDT 0.005 mg/mL".

docs.title: Skin prick test (SPT)
docs.text:
Reagent Concentration Test solution Neat (0.5 mg/mL) Positive control Histamine 10 mg/mL Negative control Normal saline

Interpretation: Wheal ≥3 mm vs negative control = positive.

docs.title: Dilution series
docs.text:
Step Dilution Concentration Preparation 1 1:100 0.005 mg/mL 0.1 mL of 0.05 mg/mL + 0.9 mL NS 2 1:10 0.05 mg/mL 0.1 mL neat + 0.9 mL NS

Inject: 0.05 mL intradermal. Read at 15–20 minutes.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Field Detail Drug class Beta-lactam / aminopenicillin Route IV (for SPT/IDT); oral (for challenge) Stock vial 500 mg powder for injection Reconstituted concentration 50 mg/mL (add 10 mL normal saline) Diluent Normal saline (0.9% sodium chloride) Storage Room temperature before reconstitution; 2–8°C after; use within 24 hours

docs.title: Preparation
docs.text:
Before you start

Check the expiry date and vial integrity before opening. Label every syringe immediately after drawing up — never leave unlabelled syringes on the tray.

docs.title: Equipment needed
docs.text:
• 1 × Amoxicillin 500 mg powder vial
  • 1 × 10 mL syringe (for reconstitution)
  • Normal saline (0.9% sodium chloride) — at least 15 mL
  • 1 mL syringes × 4 (one for SPT, three for IDT dilutions)
  • 21 G needle (for vial entry)
  • 25 G needles (for drawing up dilutions)
  • Labels or marker pen

docs.title: Step 1 — Reconstitute the stock vial
docs.text:
1. Draw up 10 mL of normal saline into a 10 mL syringe.
  2. Inject into the Amoxicillin 500 mg vial using aseptic technique.
  3. Gently swirl the vial until the powder is completely dissolved. Do not shake.
  4. The resulting stock solution is Amoxicillin 50 mg/mL. It should appear clear to slightly yellow. Discard if cloudy, discoloured or if undissolved particles are visible.
  5. Write the date, time and your initials on the vial.

docs.title: Step 2 — Prepare the SPT solution (20 mg/mL)
docs.text:
1. Draw up 2 mL of the 50 mg/mL stock into a 5 mL syringe.
  2. Add 3 mL of normal saline. Mix gently by inverting.
  3. This gives Amoxicillin 20 mg/mL (SPT solution, 5 mL total).
  4. Label: "Amoxicillin SPT — 20 mg/mL".

docs.title: Step 3 — Prepare IDT dilutions
docs.text:
Prepare in this order, using a fresh syringe for each step.

  1. IDT 10 mg/mL (1:10): Draw 0.1 mL of neat (100 mg/mL) into a 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. Label: "Amoxicillin IDT 10 mg/mL".
  2. IDT 1 mg/mL (1:100): Draw 0.1 mL of the 10 mg/mL syringe into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. Label: "Amoxicillin IDT 1 mg/mL".

docs.title: Skin prick test (SPT)
docs.text:
Reagent Concentration Test solution Neat (100 mg/mL) Positive control Histamine 10 mg/mL Negative control Normal saline

Interpretation: Wheal ≥3 mm vs negative control = positive.

docs.title: Dilution series
docs.text:
Step Dilution Concentration Preparation 1 1:100 1 mg/mL 0.1 mL of 10 mg/mL + 0.9 mL NS 2 1:10 10 mg/mL 0.1 mL neat + 0.9 mL NS

Inject: 0.02–0.05 mL intradermal. Read at 15–20 minutes.

docs.title: Oral graded dose protocol
docs.text:
Preparation: Amoxicillin oral suspension 125 mg/5 mL (25 mg/mL)

Step Dose Volume Interval 1 12.5 mg 0.5 mL 30 min 2 25 mg 1 mL 30 min 3 50 mg 2 mL 30 min 4 125 mg 5 mL 30 min 5 250 mg 10 mL 60 min observation

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Observe a minimum of 30–60 minutes after the final dose.
  • Have adrenaline drawn up and labelled before commencing.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Field Detail Drug class NSAID / Antiplatelet agent Formulation 300 mg tablets (Soluble) Concentration 10 mg/mL (Dissolve 300 mg in 30 mL water) Storage Room temp

docs.title: Oral Graded Challenge (OGC)
docs.text:
Preparation

Dissolve one 300 mg soluble Aspirin tablet into 30 mL of water to achieve a concentration of 10 mg/mL. Use this solution for the initial steps or use fractional tablets.

docs.title: Protocol
docs.text:
Step Dose Formulation Cumulative Dose 1 30 mg 3.0 mL of 10 mg/mL solution 30 mg 2 60 mg 6.0 mL of 10 mg/mL solution 90 mg 3 150 mg 1/2 Tablet 240 mg 4 300 mg 1 Tablet 540 mg 5 600 mg 2 Tablets 1140 mg

Interval: 60–90 minutes between steps as per clinical orders.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Aspirin-exacerbated respiratory disease (AERD) requires cautious challenge protocols.
  • Monitor for asthma, rhinitis, or urticaria/angioedema during the challenge.
  • If the patient is on a leukotriene antagonist (e.g., Montelukast), this should be noted as it may mask respiratory symptoms.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Field Detail Drug class Beta-lactam / aminopenicillin + beta-lactamase inhibitor Route IV (for SPT/IDT); oral suspension (for challenge) Stock vial (IV) 1.2 g powder for injection (1,000 mg amoxicillin + 200 mg clavulanate) Reconstituted concentration 60 mg/mL amoxicillin + 10 mg/mL clavulanate (add 20 mL water for injection) Oral suspension Augmentin Duo 400/57 mg per 5 mL (80 mg/mL amox + 11.4 mg/mL clav) Diluent Water for Injection (WFI) Storage Use within 30 minutes of reconstitution at room temperature

docs.title: Preparation
docs.text:
Before you start

Check the expiry date and vial integrity before opening. Label every syringe immediately after drawing up — never leave unlabelled syringes on the tray.

docs.title: Equipment needed
docs.text:
• 1 × Augmentin 1.2 g powder vial
  • 1 × 20 mL syringe (for reconstitution)
  • Water for Injection (WFI) — 20 mL
  • Normal saline (0.9% sodium chloride) — at least 10 mL (for dilutions)
  • 1 mL syringes × 3 (one for SPT, two for IDT)
  • 21 G needle (for vial entry)
  • 25 G needles (for drawing up)
  • Labels or marker pen

docs.title: Step 1 — Reconstitute the stock vial
docs.text:
1. Draw up 20 mL of Water for Injection (WFI) into a 20 mL syringe.
  2. Inject into the Augmentin 1.2 g vial using aseptic technique.
  3. Gently swirl until fully dissolved. Do not shake.
  4. The resulting stock solution is Amoxicillin 60 mg/mL + Clavulanate 10 mg/mL. It should appear clear to pale yellow. Discard if cloudy or if particles are visible.
  5. Write the date, time and your initials on the vial.

docs.title: Step 2 — Prepare the SPT solution (neat — 60 mg/mL amox)
docs.text:
1. Draw up 0.5–1 mL of the reconstituted stock directly into a 1 mL syringe.
  2. This is used neat for the skin prick test.
  3. Label: "Augmentin SPT — 60/10 mg/mL".

docs.title: Step 3 — Prepare IDT dilutions
docs.text:
Prepare in this order, using a fresh syringe for each step.

  1. IDT 20 mg/2 mg per mL (1:5): Use the 1:5 solution prepared in Step 2. Label: "Augmentin IDT 20/2 mg/mL".
  2. IDT 2 mg/0.2 mg per mL (1:50): Draw 0.1 mL of the 20/2 solution (from Step 2) into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. Label: "Augmentin IDT 2/0.2 mg/mL".

docs.title: Skin prick test (SPT)
docs.text:
Reagent Concentration Test solution 20 mg/2 mg per mL (1:5) Positive control Histamine 10 mg/mL Negative control Normal saline

Interpretation: Wheal ≥3 mm vs negative control = positive.

docs.title: Dilution series
docs.text:
Step Dilution Concentration Preparation 1 1:50 2 mg/0.2 mg per mL 0.1 mL of 1:5 solution + 0.9 mL NS 2 1:5 20 mg/2 mg per mL 0.2 mL stock + 0.8 mL NS

Inject: 0.05 mL intradermal. Read at 15–20 minutes.

docs.title: Oral graded dose protocol
docs.text:
Preparation: Augmentin Duo 400/57 suspension — shake well before each dose.

Step Dose Volume Interval 1 25/3.6 mg 0.3 mL 30 min 2 100/14.3 mg 1.25 mL 30 min 3 275/39.4 mg 3.44 mL 30 min 4 875/125 mg 10.9 mL 60 min observation

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Augmentin contains amoxicillin plus clavulanate — both components should be noted in documentation.
  • Observe a minimum of 30–60 minutes after the final dose.
  • Have adrenaline drawn up and labelled before commencing.

docs.title: Oral Graded Challenge (OGC)
docs.text:
Suspension Preparation

Use Bactrim Suspension (40 mg / 200 mg per 5 mL) which provides 8 mg / 40 mg per 1 mL.

docs.title: Protocol
docs.text:
Step Dose (TMP/SMX) Volume (Suspension) Cumulative Dose 1 16 mg / 80 mg 2.0 mL 16 mg / 80 mg 2 64 mg / 320 mg 8.0 mL 80 mg / 400 mg 3 80 mg / 400 mg 10.0 mL 160 mg / 800 mg

Interval: 30–60 minutes between steps as per clinical orders.

docs.title: Oral Ungraded Challenge
docs.text: For patients with a low-risk history or as a final validation step.

docs.title: Protocol
docs.text: Step Dose (TMP/SMX) Formulation Cumulative Dose 1 80 mg / 400 mg 1 × Tablet 80 mg / 400 mg

docs.title: Intravenous Skin Testing
docs.text: Note: IV Bactrim skin testing is less common and protocols vary based on clinical need. Consult senior staff if IV testing is required.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Field Detail Drug class Penicillin (natural beta-lactam) Route IV Stock vial 600 mg powder for injection Final SPT/IDT concentration 6 mg/mL (via multi-step dilution — see Preparation) Diluent Normal saline (0.9% sodium chloride) Storage Use within 24 hours at 2–8°C

docs.title: Preparation
docs.text:
Multi-step reconstitution required

Benzylpenicillin cannot be used directly — it requires a specific multi-step dilution to reach the working concentration of 6 mg/mL. Follow the steps below carefully.

Before you start

Check the expiry date and vial integrity before opening. Label every syringe immediately after drawing up — never leave unlabelled syringes on the tray.

docs.title: Equipment needed
docs.text:
• 1 × Benzylpenicillin 600 mg powder vial
  • Normal saline (0.9% sodium chloride) — at least 25 mL
  • Syringes: 1 × 2 mL, 2 × 10 mL, 4 × 1 mL
  • 21 G needles (for vial entry and transfers)
  • 25 G needles (for drawing up final dilutions)
  • Labels or marker pen

docs.title: Step 1 — Initial reconstitution → 300 mg/mL
docs.text:
1. Draw up 1.6 mL of normal saline into a 2 mL syringe.
  2. Inject into the Benzylpenicillin 600 mg vial. Gently swirl until dissolved.
  3. This gives Benzylpenicillin approx. 300 mg/mL. Label the vial: "BenPen 300 mg/mL".

docs.title: Step 2 — First dilution → 30 mg/mL
docs.text:
1. Draw up 1 mL of the 300 mg/mL solution into a clean 10 mL syringe.
  2. Add 9 mL of normal saline. Mix gently.
  3. This gives Benzylpenicillin 30 mg/mL. Label the syringe: "BenPen 30 mg/mL".

docs.title: Step 3 — Second dilution → 6 mg/mL (SPT/IDT neat)
docs.text:
1. Draw up 1 mL of the 30 mg/mL solution into a clean 10 mL syringe.
  2. Add 4 mL of normal saline. Mix gently.
  3. This gives Benzylpenicillin 6 mg/mL — this is the neat (undiluted) solution used for SPT.
  4. Label the syringe: "BenPen 6 mg/mL — SPT solution".

docs.title: Step 4 — Prepare IDT dilutions
docs.text:
Prepare in this order, using a fresh syringe for each step.

  1. IDT 0.6 mg/mL (1:10): Draw 1 mL of the 6 mg/mL solution into a 10 mL syringe. Add 9 mL NS. Mix. Label: "Benzylpenicillin IDT 0.6 mg/mL".
  2. IDT 0.06 mg/mL (1:100): Draw 0.1 mL of the 0.6 mg/mL solution into a 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. Label: "Benzylpenicillin IDT 0.06 mg/mL".

docs.title: Skin prick test (SPT)
docs.text:
Reagent Concentration Test solution Neat (6 mg/mL) Positive control Histamine 10 mg/mL Negative control Normal saline

Interpretation: Wheal ≥3 mm vs negative control = positive.

docs.title: Dilution series
docs.text:
Step Dilution Concentration Preparation 1 1:100 0.06 mg/mL 0.1 mL of 0.6 mg/mL + 0.9 mL NS 2 1:10 0.6 mg/mL 1 mL of 6 mg/mL (from Step 3) + 9 mL NS

Inject: 0.05 mL intradermal. Read at 15–20 minutes.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Unique multi-step reconstitution — follow the mixing guide carefully and in order.
  • Often tested alongside PPL and MD determinants as part of a full penicillin workup.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Field Detail Drug class Amide local anaesthetic Stock vial 50 mg/20 mL (2.5 mg/mL) or 0.25% solution Reconstituted conc. N/A (Ready to use) Storage Room temp; Do not freeze

docs.title: Preparation
docs.text:
Before you start

Ensure the vial contains Bupivacaine 0.25% (2.5 mg/mL). Check if it contains adrenaline (epinephrine) — for allergy testing, plain Bupivacaine is preferred.

docs.title: Equipment needed
docs.text:
• 1 × Bupivacaine 2.5 mg/mL vial (Plain)
  • Normal saline (NS) — for IDT dilutions
  • 1 mL syringes × 5 (for test doses/dilutions)
  • 25 G needles

docs.title: Step 1 — Prepare the SPT solution (2.5 mg/mL — neat)
docs.text:
1. Draw up 0.5–1 mL of the stock (2.5 mg/mL) into a 1 mL syringe.
  2. Label: "Bupivacaine SPT 2.5 mg/mL".

docs.title: Step 2 — Prepare IDT dilutions
docs.text:
Prepare in this order, using a fresh syringe for each step.

  1. IDT 0.0025 mg/mL (1:1,000):

    • A: Draw 0.1 mL of stock (2.5 mg/mL) into a 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.25 mg/mL — 1:10).
    • B: Draw 0.1 mL of A (0.25 mg/mL) into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.025 mg/mL — 1:100).
    • C: Draw 0.1 mL of B (0.025 mg/mL) into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.0025 mg/mL — 1:1,000).
    • Label: "Bupivacaine IDT 0.0025 mg/mL".

  2. IDT 0.025 mg/mL (1:100):

    • Draw 0.1 mL of syringe B (0.025 mg/mL) into a new 1 mL syringe.
    • Label: "Bupivacaine IDT 0.025 mg/mL".

  3. IDT 0.25 mg/mL (1:10):

    • Draw 0.1 mL of syringe A (0.25 mg/mL) into a new 1 mL syringe.
    • Label: "Bupivacaine IDT 0.25 mg/mL".

  4. IDT 2.5 mg/mL (Neat):

    • Draw up a fresh 0.5 mL of stock (2.5 mg/mL) into a 1 mL syringe.
    • Label: "Bupivacaine IDT 2.5 mg/mL — Neat".

docs.title: Skin prick test (SPT)
docs.text:
Reagent Concentration Test solution Neat (2.5 mg/mL) Positive control Histamine 10 mg/mL Negative control Normal saline

Interpretation: Wheal ≥3 mm vs negative control = positive.

docs.title: Dilution series
docs.text:
Step Dilution Concentration Preparation 1 1:1,000 0.0025 mg/mL 0.1 mL of 0.025 mg/mL + 0.9 mL NS 2 1:100 0.025 mg/mL 0.1 mL of 0.25 mg/mL + 0.9 mL NS 3 1:10 0.25 mg/mL 0.1 mL neat + 0.9 mL NS 4 Neat 2.5 mg/mL Undiluted stock

Inject: 0.05 mL intradermal. Read at 15–20 minutes.

Reagent Concentration Test solution Neat (100 mg/mL) Positive control Histamine 10 mg/mL Negative control Normal saline

docs.title: Dilution series

Step Dilution Concentration Preparation 1 1:100 1 mg/mL 0.1 mL of 10 mg/mL + 0.9 mL NS 2 1:10 10 mg/mL 0.1 mL neat + 0.9 mL NS

docs.title: IV graded dose protocol
docs.text:
Step Dose Interval 1 100 mg 15 min 2 200 mg 15 min 3 300 mg 15 min 4 400 mg 60 min observation

(Administered as slow IV push or infusion)

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cefazolin is the preferred pre-operative prophylactic antibiotic and the most commonly tested cephalosporin in this department.

docs.text: Step Dose Volume Cumulative Dose 1 25 mg 1.0 mL 25 mg 2 100 mg 4.0 mL 125 mg

docs.text: Intervals: Observe for 30–60 minutes between each step. Final Observation: Monitor for 1–2 hours after the final dose.

docs.title: Preparation
docs.text:
Complex Preparation

Flucloxacillin requires a precise 4-step preparation process to reach the correct testing concentrations. Do not use the initial 100 mg/mL solution for testing.

docs.title: Equipment needed
docs.text:
• 1 × Flucloxacillin 500 mg vial
  • Normal saline (NS) — for all dilutions
  • 10 mL syringe (for initial reconstitution)
  • 1 mL syringes × 5 (for serial dilutions)
  • 25 G needles

docs.title: 1. Initial Reconstitution (100 mg/mL)
docs.text:
• Add 4.6 mL NS to the 500 mg vial.
  • Dissolve completely to obtain 100 mg/mL.
  • Note: This solution is NOT for testing.

docs.title: 2. Intermediate Dilution (20 mg/mL)
docs.text:
• Draw 1.0 mL of the 100 mg/mL solution into a 1 mL or 5 mL syringe.
  • Add 4.0 mL NS. Mix.
  • This results in 20 mg/mL.
  • Note: This solution is NOT for testing.

docs.title: 3. Prepare the IDT "Neat" (2 mg/mL)
docs.text:
• Draw 0.1 mL of the 20 mg/mL solution into a 1 mL syringe.
  • Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix.
  • This results in 2 mg/mL.
  • Label: "Flucloxacillin IDT 2 mg/mL (Neat)".

docs.title: 4. Prepare the SPT and IDT 1:10 (0.2 mg/mL)
docs.text:
• Draw 0.1 mL of the 2 mg/mL solution into a new 1 mL syringe.
  • Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix.
  • This results in 0.2 mg/mL.
  • Label: "Flucloxacillin SPT/IDT 0.2 mg/mL".

docs.title: Skin prick test (SPT)
docs.text:
Reagent Concentration Test solution 0.2 mg/mL Positive control Histamine 10 mg/mL Negative control Normal saline

Interpretation: Wheal ≥3 mm vs negative control = positive.

docs.title: Dilution series
docs.text:
Step Dilution Concentration Preparation 1 1:100 0.2 mg/mL 0.1 mL of 2 mg/mL + 0.9 mL NS 2 1:10 2 mg/mL 0.1 mL neat (20mg/ml) + 0.9 mL NS

Note: The dilution ratios are relative to the 2 mg/mL IDT starting concentration.

Inject: 0.05 mL intradermal. Read at 15–20 minutes.

Intradermal testing with latex is generally not recommended due to the high risk of systemic anaphylaxis and the high sensitivity of skin prick testing for latex allergy.

1. Draw up 0.5–1 mL of the stock (5 mg/mL) into a 1 mL syringe.
  2. Label: "Metronidazole SPT 5 mg/mL".

docs.title: Step 2 — Prepare IDT dilutions
docs.text:
Prepare in this order, using a fresh syringe for each step.

  1. IDT 0.05 mg/mL (1:100):
    • Draw 0.1 mL of stock (5 mg/mL) into a 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.5 mg/mL — 1:10).
    • Draw 0.1 mL of this 0.5 mg/mL into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.05 mg/mL — 1:100).
    • Label: "Metronidazole IDT 0.05 mg/mL".

Before you start

Ensure the vial contains 10 mg/mL. Check expiry and integrity. Note that Morphine is a Schedule 8 (S8) medication and requires appropriate handling and witnessing. SPT is NOT performed neat for Morphine as it is a potent histamine-releaser.

docs.title: Step 1 — Prepare the SPT solution (1 mg/mL — 1:10)
docs.text:
1. Draw up 0.1 mL of the stock (10 mg/mL) into a 1 mL syringe.
  2. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 1 mg/mL — 1:10).
  3. Label: "Morphine SPT 1 mg/mL (1:10)".

docs.title: Step 2 — Prepare IDT dilutions
docs.text:
Prepare in this order, using a fresh syringe for each step.

  1. IDT 0.0001 mg/mL (1:100,000):
    • A: Draw 0.1 mL of stock (10 mg/mL) into a 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 1 mg/mL — 1:10).
    • B: Draw 0.1 mL of A (1 mg/mL) into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.1 mg/mL — 1:100).
    • C: Draw 0.1 mL of B (0.1 mg/mL) into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.01 mg/mL — 1:1,000).
    • D: Draw 0.1 mL of C (0.01 mg/mL) into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.001 mg/mL — 1:10,000).
    • E: Draw 0.1 mL of D (0.001 mg/mL) into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.0001 mg/mL — 1:100,000).
    • Label: "Morphine IDT 0.0001 mg/mL".

docs.title: Skin prick test (SPT)
docs.text:
Reagent Concentration Test solution 1:10 (1 mg/mL) Positive control Histamine 10 mg/mL Negative control Normal saline

Interpretation: Wheal ≥3 mm vs negative control = positive.

docs.title: Dilution series
docs.text:
Step Dilution Concentration Preparation 1 1:100,000 0.0001 mg/mL 0.1 mL of 0.001 mg/mL + 0.9 mL NS

Potent Histamine Releaser

Morphine is a potent direct histamine releaser. Results must be interpreted with caution. IDT should be performed at very high dilutions (1:100,000) as per the institutional protocol.

Inject: 0.05 mL intradermal. Read at 15–20 minutes.

PPI testing is typically performed via Oral Graded Challenge (OGC). Skin testing (SPT) with reconstituted oral formulations (Neat) may be performed but has limited validated sensitivity.

docs.text:
Step Dose Formulation Cumulative Dose 1 2 mg 1/10 Tablet (or liquid equiv) 2 mg 2 20 mg 1 Tablet 22 mg

Preparation: Dissolve 20 mg in 1 mL NS. Use the supernatant for SPT. Interpretation: Wheal ≥3 mm vs negative control = positive.

docs.title: Preparation
docs.text:
Reconstitution

Pantoprazole is reconstituted to 4 mg/mL for skin testing. Ensure the 10 mL of Normal Saline (NS) is added and the solution is clear before use.

docs.title: Equipment needed
docs.text:
• 1 × Pantoprazole 40 mg powder vial
  • Normal saline (NS) — for reconstitution and dilutions
  • 10 mL syringe
  • 1 mL syringes × 4 (for test doses/dilutions)
  • 25 G needles

docs.title: Step 1 — Reconstitute the stock (4 mg/mL)
docs.text:
1. Add 10 mL of NS to the 40 mg powder vial.
  2. Shake gently until completely dissolved. (This is 4 mg/mL).
  3. Draw up 0.5–1 mL into a 1 mL syringe.
  4. Label: "Pantoprazole SPT 4 mg/mL".

docs.title: Step 2 — Prepare IDT dilutions
docs.text:
Prepare in this order, using a fresh syringe for each step.

  1. IDT 0.4 mg/mL (1:10):

    • Draw 0.1 mL of stock (4 mg/mL) into a 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.4 mg/mL — 1:10).
    • Label: "Pantoprazole IDT 0.4 mg/mL".

  2. IDT 0.04 mg/mL (1:100):

    • Draw 0.1 mL of the 0.4 mg/mL syringe into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.04 mg/mL — 1:100).
    • Label: "Pantoprazole IDT 0.04 mg/mL".

  3. IDT 0.004 mg/mL (1:1,000):

    • Draw 0.1 mL of the 0.04 mg/mL syringe into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.004 mg/mL — 1:1,000).
    • Label: "Pantoprazole IDT 0.004 mg/mL".

docs.title: Skin prick test (SPT)
docs.text:
Reagent Concentration Test solution Neat (4 mg/mL) Positive control Histamine 10 mg/mL Negative control Normal saline

Interpretation: Wheal ≥3 mm vs negative control = positive.

docs.title: Dilution series
docs.text:
Step Dilution Concentration Preparation 1 1:1,000 0.004 mg/mL 0.1 mL of 0.04 mg/mL + 0.9 mL NS 2 1:100 0.04 mg/mL 0.1 mL of 0.4 mg/mL + 0.9 mL NS 3 1:10 0.4 mg/mL 0.1 mL neat + 0.9 mL NS

Higher Concentrations

Testing with concentrations >0.4 mg/mL may cause false-positive (irritant) results and is generally avoided unless clinically indicated.

Inject: 0.05 mL intradermal. Read at 15–20 minutes.

docs.title: Preparation
docs.text:
Before you start

PPL is often supplied as a ready-to-use or minimally reconstituted kit. Check expiry and integrity.

docs.title: Step 1 — Preparation
docs.text:
1. Follow manufacturer instructions to reconstitute if necessary with the provided diluent.
  2. The standard RPAH testing concentration is 0.04 mg/20.00 mg per mL (Neat).

docs.title: Step 2 — Prepare the SPT solution (Neat)
docs.text:
1. Draw up 0.1–0.2 mL of the neat solution.
  2. Label: "PPL SPT (Neat)".

docs.title: Step 3 — Prepare IDT dilutions
docs.text: 1. IDT 1:10 (0.004 mg/2 mg per mL): Draw 0.1 mL of neat PPL into a 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL of normal saline. Mix. Label: "PPL IDT 1:10".

docs.title: Skin prick test (SPT)
docs.text:
Reagent Concentration Test solution Neat (0.04/20.00 mg/mL) Positive control Histamine 10 mg/mL Negative control Normal saline

Interpretation: Wheal ≥3 mm vs negative control = positive.

docs.title: Dilution series
docs.text:
Step Dilution Concentration Preparation 1 1:10 0.004/2 mg per mL 0.1 mL neat + 0.9 mL normal saline 2 Neat 0.04/20.00 mg per mL Undiluted

Inject: 0.05 mL intradermal. Read at 15–20 minutes.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Use phosphate-buffered saline as diluent — not plain normal saline.
  • Typically tested alongside Penicillin Minor (MD) and Amoxicillin as part of a full penicillin allergy workup.

docs.text: Field Detail Drug class Natural penicillin / Phenoxypenicillin Route Oral Forms 250 mg, 500 mg tablets; 150 mg/5 mL, 250 mg/5 mL suspension Storage Room temp before; refrigerate suspension after mixing

docs.title: Preparation for Oral Challenge
docs.text:
Before you start

Ensure the patient has fasted if required. Use a calibrated oral syringe for all volumes. Check the concentration of the suspension (150 mg/5 mL or 250 mg/5 mL).

docs.title: Reagents
docs.text:
• Phenoxymethylpenicillin Suspension (e.g., Cilicaine VK)
  • Syringes: 1 mL, 3 mL, 5 mL, 10 mL oral syringes
  • Measuring cup

docs.title: Option A: Using 150 mg/5 mL (30 mg/mL) Suspension:
docs.text: Step Dose Volume Cumulative Dose 1 30 mg 1.0 mL 30 mg 2 60 mg 2.0 mL 90 mg 3 120 mg 4.0 mL 210 mg 4 300 mg 10.0 mL 510 mg

docs.title: Option B: Using 250 mg/5 mL (50 mg/mL) Suspension:
docs.text: Step Dose Volume Cumulative Dose 1 30 mg 0.6 mL 30 mg 2 60 mg 1.2 mL 90 mg 3 120 mg 2.4 mL 210 mg 4 300 mg 6.0 mL 510 mg

docs.title: Challenge Protocol
docs.text: Intervals: Observe for 30–60 minutes between each step. Final Observation: Monitor for 1–2 hours after the final dose.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:

docs.title: Preparation
docs.text:
Skin Irritation

Povidone iodine is a known skin irritant. Ensure the negative control (normal saline) is read carefully. Testing is typically performed with aqueous solutions, not alcoholic tinctures.

docs.title: Equipment needed
docs.text:
• 1 × Povidone Iodine 10% aqueous solution
  • Normal saline (NS) — for IDT dilutions
  • 1 mL syringes × 5 (for test doses/dilutions)
  • 25 G needles

docs.title: Step 1 — Prepare the SPT solution (10% — neat)
docs.text:
1. Draw up 0.5–1 mL of the stock (10%) into a 1 mL syringe.
  2. Label: "Povidone Iodine SPT 10%".

docs.title: Step 2 — Prepare IDT dilutions
docs.text:
Prepare in this order, using a fresh syringe for each step.

  1. IDT 0.01 mg/mL (1:10,000):
    • Draw 0.1 mL of stock (100 mg/mL) into a 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 10 mg/mL — 1:10).
    • Draw 0.1 mL of this 10 mg/mL into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 1 mg/mL — 1:100).
    • Draw 0.1 mL of this 1 mg/mL into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.1 mg/mL — 1:1,000).
    • Draw 0.1 mL of this 0.1 mg/mL into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.01 mg/mL — 1:10,000).
    • Label: "Povidone Iodine IDT 0.01 mg/mL".

docs.title: Skin prick test (SPT)
docs.text:
Reagent Concentration Test solution Neat (10%) Positive control Histamine 10 mg/mL Negative control Normal saline

Interpretation: Wheal ≥3 mm vs negative control = positive.

docs.text:
Step Dilution Concentration Preparation 1 1:10,000 0.01 mg/mL 0.1 mL of 0.1 mg/mL + 0.9 mL NS

Inject: 0.05 mL intradermal. Read at 15–20 minutes. Delayed Reading: If history is suggestive of a delayed reaction (e.g., contact dermatitis), re-read at 24 and 48 hours.

Testing Method

PPI testing is typically performed via Oral Graded Challenge (OGC). Skin testing (SPT) with reconstituted oral formulations (40 mg/mL Neat) may be performed but has limited validated sensitivity.

docs.text:
Step Dose Formulation Cumulative Dose 1 2 mg 1/10 Tablet (or liquid equiv) 2 mg 2 20 mg 1 Tablet 22 mg

docs.title: Step 2 — Prepare IDT dilutions
docs.text:
Prepare in this order, using a fresh syringe for each step.

  1. IDT 0.002 mg/mL (1:1,000):

    • A: Draw 0.1 mL of stock (2 mg/mL) into a 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.2 mg/mL — 1:10).
    • B: Draw 0.1 mL of A (0.2 mg/mL) into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.02 mg/mL — 1:100).
    • C: Draw 0.1 mL of B (0.02 mg/mL) into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.002 mg/mL — 1:1,000).
    • Label: "Ropivacaine IDT 0.002 mg/mL".

  2. IDT 0.02 mg/mL (1:100):

    • Draw 0.1 mL of syringe B (0.02 mg/mL) into a new 1 mL syringe.
    • Label: "Ropivacaine IDT 0.02 mg/mL".

  3. IDT 0.2 mg/mL (1:10):

    • Draw 0.1 mL of syringe A (0.2 mg/mL) into a new 1 mL syringe.
    • Label: "Ropivacaine IDT 0.2 mg/mL".

  4. IDT 2 mg/mL (Neat):

    • Draw up a fresh 0.5 mL of stock (2 mg/mL) into a 1 mL syringe.
    • Label: "Ropivacaine IDT 2 mg/mL — Neat".

docs.title: Preparation (Desensitisation)
docs.text:
Protocol Context

Rosuvastatin desensitisation is typically performed for patients with a history of non-severe hypersensitivity who require statin therapy. This protocol uses standard 10 mg tablets.

docs.title: Equipment needed
docs.text:
• 3 × Rosuvastatin 10 mg tablets
  • Water for swallowing
  • Observation chart

docs.title: Dosing Schedule
docs.text:
Step Dose Formulation Cumulative Dose 1 10 mg 1 × Tablet 10 mg 2 10 mg 1 × Tablet 20 mg 3 10 mg 1 × Tablet 30 mg

Interval: 30 minutes between doses. Total Dose: 30 mg.

Post-Protocol

If the desensitisation is successful, the patient should continue their prescribed daily dose (e.g., 10 mg or 20 mg) starting the following day without interruption.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Monitor for signs of hypersensitivity (rash, itch, respiratory distress) between steps.
  • This protocol is specific for immediate-type reactions. For delayed muscle symptoms (SAMS), a different clinical approach is required.

docs.text:
1. Add 20 mL NS to the 4.5 g (4 g/500 mg) vial.

  3. Total concentration is Piperacillin 20 mg/mL and Tazobactam 2.5 mg/mL.
  4. Label: "Tazocin SPT/IDT 20/2 mg/mL".

docs.title: Step 1 — Reconstitute the stock (25 mg/mL)
docs.text:
1. Add 20 mL of WFI to the 500 mg powder vial.

  3. Draw up 0.5–1 mL into a 1 mL syringe.

    • Draw 0.1 mL of the 2.5 mg/mL syringe into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.25 mg/mL — 1:100).

docs.title: Step 1 — Prepare the SPT solution (300 mg I/mL — neat)
docs.text:
1. Draw up 0.5–1 mL of the stock (300 mg I/mL) into a 1 mL syringe.
  2. Label: "Ultravist SPT 300 mg I/mL".

docs.title: Step 2 — Prepare IDT dilutions
docs.text:
Prepare in this order, using a fresh syringe for each step.

  1. IDT 1:10 (30 mg I/mL):

    • Draw 0.1 mL of stock (300 mg I/mL) into a 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 30 mg I/mL — 1:10).
    • Label: "Ultravist IDT 1:10".

  2. IDT 1:100 (3 mg I/mL):

    • Draw 0.1 mL of the 1:10 syringe into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 3 mg I/mL — 1:100).
    • Label: "Ultravist IDT 1:100".

docs.title: Skin prick test (SPT)
docs.text:
Reagent Concentration Test solution Neat (300 mg I/mL) Positive control Histamine 10 mg/mL Negative control Normal saline

Interpretation: Wheal ≥3 mm vs negative control = positive.

docs.title: Dilution series
docs.text:
Step Dilution Concentration Preparation 1 1:100 3 mg I/mL 0.1 mL of 1:10 + 0.9 mL NS 2 1:10 30 mg I/mL 0.1 mL neat + 0.9 mL NS

Inject: 0.05 mL intradermal. Read at 15–20 minutes. Delayed Reading: If history is suggestive of a delayed reaction, re-read at 24 and 48 hours.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Field Detail Drug class Glycopeptide antibiotic Stock vial 500 mg or 1 g powder for injection Reconstituted conc. 100 mg/mL (add 5 mL NS to 500 mg or 10 mL NS to 1 g) Storage Room temp before; 2–8°C after

docs.title: Preparation
docs.text:
Before you start

Ensure the vial contains 500 mg or 1 g of Vancomycin. Check expiry and integrity. Note that Vancomycin is a potent direct histamine-releaser and must be tested at extremely high dilutions.

docs.title: Equipment needed
docs.text:
• 1 × Vancomycin 500 mg or 1 g vial
  • Normal saline (NS) — for all dilutions
  • 10 mL syringe (for initial reconstitution)
  • 1 mL syringes × 10 (for multiple serial dilutions)
  • 25 G needles

docs.title: Step 1 — Initial Reconstitution (100 mg/mL)
docs.text:
1. Reconstitute the 500 mg vial with 5 mL NS (or 1 g vial with 10 mL NS).
  2. Dissolve completely to obtain 100 mg/mL.
  3. Note: This solution is NOT for testing.

docs.title: Step 2 — Prepare the SPT solution (100 mg/mL)
docs.text:
1. Draw up 0.5–1 mL of the neat (100 mg/mL) solution into a 1 mL syringe.
  2. Label: "Vancomycin SPT 100 mg/mL".

docs.title: Step 3 — Prepare IDT dilutions (1:1,000,000)
docs.text:
Prepare in this order, using a fresh syringe for each step.

  1. IDT 0.0001 mg/mL (1:1,000,000):
    • A: Draw 0.1 mL of stock (100 mg/mL) into a 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 10 mg/mL — 1:10).
    • B: Draw 0.1 mL of A (10 mg/mL) into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 1 mg/mL — 1:100).
    • C: Draw 0.1 mL of B (1 mg/mL) into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.1 mg/mL — 1:1,000).
    • D: Draw 0.1 mL of C (0.1 mg/mL) into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.01 mg/mL — 1:10,000).
    • E: Draw 0.1 mL of D (0.01 mg/mL) into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.001 mg/mL — 1:100,000).
    • F: Draw 0.1 mL of E (0.001 mg/mL) into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.0001 mg/mL — 1:1,000,000).
    • Label: "Vancomycin IDT 0.0001 mg/mL".

docs.text:
Reagent Concentration Test solution Neat (100 mg/mL) Positive control Histamine 10 mg/mL Negative control Normal saline

Interpretation: Wheal ≥3 mm vs negative control = positive.

docs.title: Dilution series
docs.text:
Step Dilution Concentration Preparation 1 1:1,000,000 0.0001 mg/mL 0.1 mL of 0.001 mg/mL + 0.9 mL NS

Histamine Release

Vancomycin is a potent direct histamine releaser. Results must be interpreted with extreme caution. IDT should be performed at 1:1,000,000 as per the institutional protocol.

Inject: 0.05 mL intradermal. Read at 15–20 minutes.

docs.text: Field Detail Drug class Neuromuscular blocking agent (NMBA) Stock vial 10 mg powder for injection Reconstituted conc. 4 mg/mL (add 2.5 mL WFI) Storage Room temp before; 2–8°C after

docs.title: Preparation
docs.text:
Before you start

Ensure the vial contains 10 mg of Vecuronium. Check expiry and integrity. Note that this requires 2.5 mL of diluent.

docs.title: Equipment needed
docs.text:
• 1 × Vecuronium 10 mg vial
  • Water for injection (WFI) — at least 2.5 mL
  • Normal saline (NS) — for IDT dilutions
  • 5 mL syringe (for reconstitution)
  • 1 mL syringes × 4 (for test doses)
  • 25 G needles

docs.title: Step 1 — Reconstitution
docs.text:
1. Reconstitute the 10 mg vial with 2.5 mL WFI.
  2. Dissolve completely to obtain Vecuronium 4 mg/mL (Neat).

docs.title: Step 2 — Prepare the SPT solution (4 mg/mL — neat)
docs.text:
1. Draw up 0.5–1 mL of the neat (4 mg/mL) solution into a 1 mL syringe.
  2. Label: "Vecuronium SPT 4 mg/mL".

docs.title: Step 3 — Prepare IDT dilutions
docs.text:
Prepare in this order, using a fresh syringe for each step.

  1. IDT 0.04 mg/mL (1:100):

    • Draw 0.1 mL of neat (4 mg/mL) into a 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.4 mg/mL — 1:10).
    • Draw 0.1 mL of this 0.4 mg/mL into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.04 mg/mL — 1:100).
    • Label: "Vecuronium IDT 0.04 mg/mL".

  2. IDT 0.004 mg/mL (1:1,000):

    • Draw 0.1 mL of the 0.04 mg/mL syringe into a new 1 mL syringe. Add 0.9 mL NS. Mix. (This is 0.004 mg/mL — 1:1,000).
    • Label: "Vecuronium IDT 0.004 mg/mL".

docs.title: Anaphylaxis management
docs.text:
2026-03-28

Emergency — act immediately

If anaphylaxis is suspected, do not delay treatment to confirm diagnosis.

docs.title: Recognition
docs.text:
Anaphylaxis is likely if any ONE of the following:

  1. Acute onset illness with skin/mucosal involvement (urticaria, flushing, angioedema) AND at least one of: respiratory compromise, hypotension, or end-organ dysfunction
  2. Two or more of the following after exposure to a likely allergen: skin/mucosal involvement, respiratory compromise, hypotension, gastrointestinal symptoms
  3. Hypotension alone after known allergen exposure

docs.title: 1. Stop the drug / remove allergen
docs.text: Cease infusion or remove SPT/IDT materials if possible.

docs.title: 2. Call for help
docs.text: Activate emergency response — call 2222 (RPAH internal emergency).

docs.title: 3. Adrenaline — first-line treatment
docs.text:
Adrenaline is the ONLY first-line treatment

Do not delay adrenaline to administer antihistamines or corticosteroids.

Adrenaline 1:1000 (1 mg/mL) — intramuscular (IM), mid-outer thigh

Patient weight Dose Volume (1:1000) < 20 kg 0.01 mg/kg 0.01 mL/kg 20–50 kg 0.3 mg 0.3 mL > 50 kg 0.5 mg 0.5 mL
  • Route: IM, mid-outer thigh (through clothing if needed)
  • Repeat: every 5 min if no improvement
  • Pre-drawn adrenaline syringe must be available before commencing any IDT or challenge

docs.title: 4. Position
docs.text:
• Lay patient flat with legs elevated (unless respiratory distress — sit upright)
  • Do not allow patient to stand or sit up suddenly

docs.title: 5. Oxygen
docs.text: High-flow O₂ via non-rebreather mask.

docs.title: 6. IV access
docs.text: Establish large-bore IV access if not already in situ. Give IV fluid bolus (NS 20 mL/kg) if hypotensive.

docs.title: 7. Adjunct medications (secondary — after adrenaline)
docs.text: Drug Dose Route Purpose Salbutamol 5 mg nebulised Inhaled Bronchospasm Promethazine 25–50 mg (adult) IM/IV slow Antihistamine (H1) Hydrocortisone 200 mg (adult) IV Prevent biphasic reaction Ranitidine 50 mg (adult) IV slow Antihistamine (H2)

docs.title: Biphasic reaction
docs.text:
• Can occur 1–72 h after initial anaphylaxis (typically 8–12 h)
  • All patients who receive adrenaline should be observed for minimum 4–6 hours
  • Discuss extended observation or admission with AMO

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text: Record in patient notes: - Time of reaction onset - Signs and symptoms observed - Adrenaline dose, route, time, and response - All subsequent medications given - Vital signs before and after treatment - Outcome and disposition plan

docs.title: References
docs.text:
• ASCIA Anaphylaxis guidelines: allergy.org.au
  • Australian Prescriber Anaphylaxis wallchart

docs.title: 28 March 2026 — Version 0.1
docs.text:
Author: RPA Allergy Nurses Change: First published version of the RPAH Drug Allergy Testing Library.

This version includes individual drug pages for all agents currently tested by the department, covering the following groups:

  • Penicillins — Amoxicillin, Ampicillin, Augmentin, Benzylpenicillin, Flucloxacillin, Penicillin Major (PPL), Penicillin Minor (MD), Phenoxymethylpenicillin
  • Cephalosporins — Cefazolin, Cefepime, Cefotaxime, Ceftazidime, Ceftriaxone, Cefuroxime, Cephalexin
  • Muscle relaxants — Cisatracurium, Pancuronium, Rocuronium, Suxamethonium, Vecuronium
  • Opioids — Alfentanil, Fentanyl, Morphine, Oxycodone, Remifentanil
  • Anaesthetic agents — Ketamine, Midazolam, Propofol, Thiopental
  • Local anaesthetics — Bupivacaine, Lignocaine, Mepivacaine, Ropivacaine
  • Proton pump inhibitors — Esomeprazole, Lansoprazole, Omeprazole, Pantoprazole, Rabeprazole
  • NSAIDs and analgesics — Aspirin, Paracetamol, Parecoxib
  • Contrast media — Omnipaque, Ultravist, Visipaque
  • Anticoagulants — Dalteparin, Enoxaparin, Heparin
  • Other antibiotics — Bactrim, Ciprofloxacin, Doxycycline, Levofloxacin, Metronidazole
  • Miscellaneous — Chlorhexidine, Dexamethasone, Fluconazole, Latex, Patent Blue, Povidone Iodine, Rosuvastatin, Sugammadex, Tranexamic Acid

Each drug page includes a stock concentration overview, skin prick test (SPT) protocol, intradermal test (IDT) dilution series, and where applicable, an oral or intravenous challenge protocol. Clinical data was sourced from the department's internal reference spreadsheets (Medication List 2021 and Med Chart 2021).

Reference pages are also included for anaphylaxis emergency management and a general mixing and dilution guide.

docs.title: Mixing & dilution guide
docs.text:
2026-03-28

General principles for drug preparation for allergy skin testing at RPAH Clinical Immunology & Allergy.

docs.title: Diluents
docs.text: Diluent Abbreviation When to use 0.9% Sodium Chloride NS / N/S Default diluent for most drugs Water for Injection WFI When specified (e.g. Benzylpenicillin, most powder vials) Saline in phosphate buffer — Penicillin determinants (PPL, MD) only Nil — Ready-to-use solutions (e.g. Rocuronium, Propofol)

docs.title: Serial dilution method
docs.text:
To make a 1:10 dilution: take 1 mL of solution + 9 mL diluent.

To make a 1:100 dilution: two options: - Direct: 0.1 mL of stock + 9.9 mL diluent - Serial: 1 mL of 1:10 dilution + 9 mL diluent

To make a 1:1,000 dilution: 1 mL of 1:100 + 9 mL diluent (and so on).

Label every syringe immediately after preparation

Include: drug name, concentration, date/time prepared, preparer initials.

docs.title: IDT volumes and technique
docs.text:
• Standard IDT volume: 0.05 mL (50 µL)
  • Use a 1 mL tuberculin syringe with a 26–27 g needle
  • Inject intradermally — a visible bleb of 3–5 mm should form
  • If no bleb forms, the injection was subcutaneous — discard and repeat at a new site

docs.title: SPT technique
docs.text:
• Apply a small drop of test solution to the volar forearm
  • Prick through the drop at 90° with a sterile lancet
  • Blot excess solution after 1 min — do not wipe
  • Positive control (histamine 10 mg/mL) and negative control (NS) must be included
  • Space test sites at least 3 cm apart
  • Read at 15–20 min

Positive SPT: Wheal ≥ 3 mm greater than the negative control

docs.title: Powder vial reconstitution reference
docs.text: Drug Vial size Add diluent Reconstituted concentration Amoxicillin 1 g 9.2 mL WFI 100 mg/mL Ampicillin 1 g 9.2 mL WFI 100 mg/mL Cefazolin 1 g 10 mL WFI 100 mg/mL Ceftazidime 2 g 20 mL WFI 100 mg/mL Cefotaxime 1 g 10 mL WFI 100 mg/mL Ceftriaxone 1 g 10 mL WFI 100 mg/mL Cefepime 1 g 10 mL WFI 100 mg/mL Benzylpenicillin 600 mg See Benzylpenicillin page 6 mg/mL (neat for testing) Vecuronium 10 mg 2.5 mL WFI 4 mg/mL Remifentanil 1 mg 20 mL WFI 0.05 mg/mL Pantoprazole 40 mg 10 mL NS 40 mg/mL

docs.title: Waste and disposal
docs.text:
• Discard all prepared solutions at end of testing session
  • Do not store reconstituted solutions beyond the times specified on individual drug pages
  • Dispose of sharps immediately into sharps container

docs.title: Pre-testing checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Written consent obtained and documented
  • [ ] Baseline vitals recorded (BP, HR, SpO₂)
  • [ ] Adrenaline 0.5 mg/mL (1:1000) drawn, labelled, and within reach
  • [ ] IV access established (for IDT and all challenges)
  • [ ] Antihistamines withheld ≥ 72 h (check with AMO if unclear)
  • [ ] Beta-blockers noted (may blunt anaphylaxis treatment response — inform AMO)
  • [ ] Resuscitation equipment checked and available
